story.iWant: a generator that can write Nikon .np3 Picture Control binary files from UniversalRecipe
story.soThat: I can convert other formats to .np3 for use with Nikon cameras
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Create Generate() function signature (AC: #1, #5) - Add Generate(*models.UniversalRecipe) ([]byte, error) to parse.go or new generate.go - Import encoding/binary for binary writing - Add function documentation
- Task 2: Implement file header generation (AC: #1) - Write magic bytes "NCP" (0x4E 0x43 0x50) at offset 0-2 - Write version bytes at offset 3-6 - Write preset name at offset 0x14-0x28 (null-terminated, 20 bytes max) - Initialize byte buffer with minimum size of 300 bytes
- Task 3: Implement parameter conversion (AC: #2) - Convert UniversalRecipe.Sharpness (0-150) to NP3 sharpening (0-9) - Convert UniversalRecipe.Contrast (-100/+100) to NP3 contrast (-3/+3) - Convert UniversalRecipe brightness to NP3 brightness (-1.0/+1.0) - Convert UniversalRecipe.Saturation (-100/+100) to NP3 saturation (-3/+3) - Convert UniversalRecipe hue to NP3 hue (-9/+9) - Document conversion formulas in code comments
- Task 4: Implement TLV chunk generation (AC: #3) - Create encodeChunk() helper function - Write chunk ID (4 bytes, little-endian uint32) - Write reserved bytes (4 bytes, zeros) - Write value length (2 bytes, little-endian uint16) - Write value data (N bytes) - Generate chunks for all parameters starting at offset 0x2C
- Task 5: Implement Generate() main logic (AC: #1-5) - Validate input UniversalRecipe is not nil - Convert parameters (Task 3 logic) - Generate file header (Task 2 logic) - Generate TLV chunks (Task 4 logic) - Return byte slice or error
- Task 6: Write comprehensive tests (AC: #6) - Create generator_test.go or extend np3_test.go - Implement TestGenerate() with table-driven pattern - Test round-trip: Parse() → Generate() → Parse() - Verify generated files have correct magic bytes - Verify generated files parse without error - Test edge cases: empty name, min/max parameter values - Run go test -cover and verify 95%+ coverage
- Task 7: Validate round-trip accuracy (AC: #4) - Load all 73 sample NP3 files - For each: Parse() → Generate() → Parse() - Compare original vs round-trip parameters - Verify parameters match within tolerance - Document any systematic differences

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
1. NP3 File Structure Generation - Generate valid NP3 files with correct magic bytes "NCP" (0x4E 0x43 0x50) - Write minimum file size of 300 bytes minimum (based on actual file analysis) - Generate correct file header with version information - Files must be loadable in Nikon NX Studio without errors
2. Parameter Conversion - Convert Sharpness from UniversalRecipe (0-150 range) to NP3 (0-9 range) - Convert Contrast from UniversalRecipe (-100 to +100) to NP3 (-3 to +3) - Convert Brightness from UniversalRecipe to NP3 (-1.0 to +1.0) - Convert Saturation from UniversalRecipe (-100 to +100) to NP3 (-3 to +3) - Convert Hue from UniversalRecipe to NP3 (-9° to +9° range) - All parameters mapped correctly from UniversalRecipe fields
3. Binary Encoding - Write preset name at offset 0x14-0x28 (null-terminated ASCII, 20 bytes) - Generate TLV (Type-Length-Value) chunks starting at offset 0x2C - Use little-endian encoding for all multi-byte values - Chunk structure: 4-byte ID, 4-byte reserved, 2-byte length, N-byte value
4. Round-Trip Validation - Parse(Generate(recipe)) should produce equivalent UniversalRecipe - Parameter values should match within acceptable tolerance - File structure must match original NP3 format - Binary output should be loadable in Nikon NX Studio
5. Error Handling - Follow Pattern 5 (Error Handling) from architecture.md - Wrap errors with format-specific context - Include operation context ("generate NP3" vs "encode parameter") - Preserve underlying error for debugging with error wrapping - No panics - all errors returned via error return value
6. Test Coverage - Generate NP3 files from all 73 sample files (parse → generate roundtrip) - Table-driven tests following architecture Pattern 7 - Verify generated files parse successfully - Achieve 95%+ code coverage for generate.go - Test edge cases: empty name, boundary parameter values - Validate binary structure of generated files

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Recipe is a universal photo preset converter with bidirectional conversion goal of 95%+ accuracy. NP3 is Nikon's proprietary binary format (undocumented, reverse-engineered). Core requirement: Generate valid NP3 files that open in Nikon NX Studio.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Always return wrapped ConversionError for conversion failures with Operation, Format, and Cause fields. All format packages must wrap errors with format-specific context using fmt.Errorf with %w verb.

doc.title: System Architecture
doc.snippet: Table-driven tests using real sample files from testdata/. For NP3: 22 sample files (updated to 73 during Story 1-2). Run subtest for each file, validate parse succeeds, verify round-trip conversions produce identical output (±1 tolerance for rounding).

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: NP3 binary format: Magic bytes "NCP" (0x4E 0x43 0x50), minimum 300 bytes (actual files 392+ bytes). TLV chunks start at offset 0x2C. Parameters: Sharpening (0-9), Contrast (-3 to +3), Brightness (-1.0 to +1.0), Saturation (-3 to +3), Hue (-9° to +9°).

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Each format package contains parse.go, generate.go, and {format}_test.go. NP3 package located at internal/formats/np3/. Must follow hub-and-spoke pattern with UniversalRecipe as intermediate representation.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Generate NP3 files that open in Nikon NX Studio without errors. Round-trip conversion (NP3 → UniversalRecipe → NP3) produces binary-identical output (±1 tolerance). Validate with all sample files from testdata/np3/ (73 files discovered in Story 1-2).

artifact.reason: Core data structure for hub-and-spoke conversion. Contains all parameters that NP3 generator must map to binary format. Key fields: Name, Contrast, Saturation, Sharpness (relevant to NP3 5-parameter format).

Parser function that converts NP3 binary to UniversalRecipe. Generator must produce output that this parser can successfully parse (round-trip validation). Validates magic bytes, file size, and parameter ranges.
NP3 generator implementation (Story 1-3, already implemented). Converts UniversalRecipe to NP3 binary format. Includes parameter conversion formulas, validation, and binary encoding logic.
Parameter conversion logic showing formula implementations: Sharpness (÷10), Contrast (÷33), Saturation (÷33). Critical for understanding inverse of parser formulas.
Round-trip test validating 73 sample files: Parse → Generate → Parse. Validates binary-identical output within ±1 tolerance. Test framework for validation strategy.

dependencies.external: None - Standard library only per architecture constraints

story-context.constraints: - Architecture Pattern 4 (File Structure): Generator must be in internal/formats/np3/generate.go following identical structure to parser - Architecture Pattern 5 (Error Handling): All errors must be wrapped with "generate NP3" context using fmt.Errorf with %w verb. No panics allowed. - Architecture Pattern 6 (Validation): Fail-fast with descriptive errors. Validate input recipe is not nil before processing. - Architecture Pattern 7 (Testing): Table-driven tests with 73 real NP3 sample files from testdata/np3/ (discovered in Story 1-2, originally 22) - Standard Library Only: Must use only encoding/binary, bytes.Buffer, fmt - no external dependencies - Binary Format Constraints: Magic bytes "NCP", minimum 300 bytes, little-endian encoding, TLV chunk structure at offset 0x2C - Parameter Range Constraints: Sharpness (0-9), Contrast (-3 to +3), Saturation (-3 to +3), Brightness (-1.0 to +1.0), Hue (-9 to +9) - Round-Trip Requirement: Parse(Generate(recipe)) must produce equivalent UniversalRecipe within ±1 tolerance for rounding - Conversion Formula Accuracy: Must use inverse of parser formulas (Sharpness: ÷10, Contrast: ÷33, Saturation: ÷33)

interface.name: UniversalRecipe
interface.signature: type UniversalRecipe struct { Name string; Contrast int; Saturation int; Sharpness int; ... }

tests.standards: Go standard library testing package (testing). Table-driven pattern with filepath.Glob to discover all sample files. Each file becomes a subtest via t.Run(fileName). Tests validate: (1) function succeeds, (2) parameters in valid ranges, (3) round-trip accuracy (Parse → Generate → Parse produces equivalent output within ±1 tolerance). Error tests verify graceful failure for invalid inputs (nil recipe, invalid parameters). Coverage goal: 95%+ achieved via go test -cover.

test.name: TestGenerate - Basic generation validation
test.description: Validate Generate() creates valid NP3 binary with magic bytes "NCP", minimum 300 bytes, correct header structure. Verify generated file can be parsed successfully by Parse().

test.name: TestGenerateNilRecipe - Error handling
test.description: Verify Generate() returns descriptive error when recipe is nil. Validates Pattern 5 (Error Handling) compliance with wrapped errors.

test.name: TestRoundTrip - Validate all 73 sample files
test.description: For each of 73 NP3 files: Parse → Generate → Parse. Compare original vs round-trip parameters. Verify Sharpness, Contrast, Saturation match within ±10, ±5, ±5 tolerance respectively. 100% success rate required.

test.name: TestGenerateEmptyName - Edge case validation
test.description: Verify Generate() handles empty recipe name gracefully. Should succeed with empty or default name in generated file.

test.name: TestGenerateBoundaryValues - Parameter range validation
test.description: Test boundary cases: Sharpness at 0 and 150, Contrast at -100 and +100, Saturation at -100 and +100. Verify conversion to NP3 ranges handles clamping correctly (Sharpness 0-9, Contrast/Saturation -3 to +3).

test.name: TestBinaryStructure - TLV chunk validation
test.description: Verify generated binary has correct TLV structure: chunks start at 0x2C, correct chunk ID (4 bytes little-endian), reserved bytes (4 zeros), length (2 bytes little-endian), value data.
